source.title: Appetitive behavior: impact of amygdala-dependent mechanisms of emotional learning
source.venue: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: Provides the canonical conceptual and empirical framework for the two-module BLA/CeA architecture that SD-035 directly implements. The review synthesises double-dissociation evidence across multiple paradigms: BLA lesions impair CS-UCS specific outcome-value associations (what SD-035's BLAAnalog does: encoding_gain and retrieval_bias weighted by outcome-valence) while CeA lesions impair general motivational/mode-prior influences on behaviour (what SD-035's CeAAnalog does: mode_prior to SalienceCoordinator). The evolutionary framing (phylogenetically recent BLA for outcome-value retrieval; phylogenetically older CeA for incentive-motivational invigoration) maps well to the peer-module architecture SD-035 registers.
failure_signatures: If BLA and CeA lesions produced identical patterns of deficit across paradigms, the two-module dissociation underpinning SD-035's peer-module design would be unsupported.

The review acknowledges that under many conditions both systems operate together; if cooperative operation requires direct BLA-to-CeA communication that SD-035 does not implement, the isolated peer architecture may miss an important coupling constraint.

mapping.source_claim_statement: BLA is required for CS-UCS specific value associations (outcome representation, current affective value access); CeA mediates stimulus-response representations and conditioned motivational influences on behaviour. These systems superimpose: BLA (phylogenetically recent) encodes outcome value; CeA (phylogenetically older) enables incentive-motivational invigoration.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-035's BLAAnalog handles encoding_gain (MECH-074a), retrieval_bias (MECH-074b), and remap_signal (MECH-074d) — the CS-UCS value modulation role. SD-035's CeAAnalog emits mode_prior to SalienceCoordinator (MECH-046) and fast_prime (MECH-074c) — the incentive-motivational/mode-prior role. The peer-module architecture mirrors the biological two-module dissociation the review establishes.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Review is based primarily on appetitive (reward) paradigms and rodents; SD-035 is implemented for harm-avoidance. The BLA-UCS value function may differ qualitatively for aversive UCS. However, the CeA mode-prior function is likely preserved across valences (both Parkinson 2000 appetitive and Walker & Davis 1997 aversive CEA experiments converge on the same CeA motivational role).
mapping.source_context: Review synthesising excitotoxic lesion studies in rats; Pavlovian conditioning, instrumental action, conditioned reinforcement, and PIT paradigms

confidence_components.notes: Prestigious authors (Everitt, Robbins group; Cambridge); Ann NY Acad Sci canonical review series. Transfer risk moderate for valence; low for species (rodent amygdala circuit topology well-conserved).
